candidate.name: Utility-scale power transformers
candidate.description: Large power transformers required for utility-scale transmission and grid expansion projects.
notes: The strongest evidence in this case points to large power transformers as the tightest transformer bottleneck.
Procurement timelines measured in years make this a slow-moving, durable chokepoint.

score.explanation: Utility-scale power transformers scores 88.8/100 (critical) as a chokepoint candidate. The strongest weighted drivers are supplier concentration, qualification friction, capacity lead time. The least supportive signals are pricing power evidence, geopolitical exposure. This score should be treated as a ranking aid, not as a trading decision on its own.

candidate.name: Distribution transformers
candidate.description: Distribution transformers used across homes, businesses, EV charging, and data-center connected load growth.
notes: Demand breadth is very strong because this layer touches homes, businesses, EV charging, and data centers.
Still looks less acute than large power transformers on the current evidence.

score.explanation: Distribution transformers scores 72.8/100 (high) as a chokepoint candidate. The strongest weighted drivers are demand acceleration, supplier concentration, qualification friction. The least supportive signals are pricing power evidence, geopolitical exposure. This score should be treated as a ranking aid, not as a trading decision on its own.

candidate.name: High-voltage cables and related grid equipment
candidate.description: High-voltage cables and adjacent transmission equipment needed for large-scale grid buildout.
notes: Cables are clearly constrained, but current evidence still points to transformers as the tighter gate.
This layer may become more important as transmission buildout broadens.

score.candidate_name: High-voltage cables and related grid equipment
score.explanation: High-voltage cables and related grid equipment scores 69.2/100 (high) as a chokepoint candidate. The strongest weighted drivers are supplier concentration, qualification friction, capacity lead time. The least supportive signals are pricing power evidence, substitutability inverse. This score should be treated as a ranking aid, not as a trading decision on its own.
